title: expose-vault-cross-cluster
summary: Implemented Vault cross-cluster exposure for the VSO two-cluster Podman demo: added an additive `vault-external` NodePort Service (port 8200 â†’ nodePort 30820, matching the existing kind `extraPortMappings`) in `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh`, and created a reusable, diagnostic-rich connectivity-check script plus unit tests; validated live end-to-end against real `kind-vault-lab`/`kind-vso-lab` Podman-backed clusters.
keyLearnings: - `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` (from task 03) already pre-declared `VAULT_NODE_PORT=30820`/`VAULT_HOST_PORT=8200`/`VAULT_ADDR=http://host.containers.internal:8200`, and `scripts/kind/vault-lab-config.yaml.tmpl` already had the matching `extraPortMappings` â€” task 05's job was purely to create the actual NodePort Service using those pre-agreed values, which I did as a new `vault-external` Service (selector `app.kubernetes.io/instance=vault,app.kubernetes.io/name=vault,component=server`, matching the vault-0 pod's real labels). This is additive and does not touch the Helm chart's own `vault`/`vault-internal` ClusterIP Services, so same-cluster consumers (Agent Injector, OTel) are unaffected.

- Discovered tasks 06 and 07 (`scripts/setup-vso-cluster.sh`, `scripts/configure-vso-kubernetes-auth.sh`) were already committed ahead of 05 by parallel/out-of-order execution â€” confirmed neither depends on the NodePort Service created here (07 handles the reverse direction: Vaultâ†’VSO API server auth via `VSO_API_ADDR`/port 6444), so no conflicts.

- `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` runs a one-shot `kubectl run --rm -i --restart=Never --image=curlimages/curl` pod in `VSO_CONTEXT`, curls `${VAULT_ADDR}/v1/sys/health`, and treats HTTP codes `200|429|472|473|501|503` as connectivity success (Vault's health endpoint intentionally returns non-200 for sealed/standby/uninitialized states â€” this is a reachability check, not a readiness check). On `HTTP_CODE=000` (curl couldn't connect at all) it prints Podman/kind networking troubleshooting steps (host gateway resolution, extraPortMappings, missing `vault-external` Service, Podman machine not running).

- Verified live: `curl http://localhost:8200/v1/sys/health` from the host returns 200, and `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` run with the real `VAULT_ADDR` returns `OK: reached Vault ... (HTTP 200)` from inside a pod in `kind-vso-lab`; also verified the failure path prints the expected diagnostics when pointed at a bad port.
- Both `scripts/setup-vault-cluster.sh` and `scripts/check-vault-connectivity.sh` are idempotent and safe to re-run against the already-live demo clusters (confirmed via repeated runs).

- Downstream tasks (VSO CRDs / VaultConnection in task 08) should use `VAULT_ADDR` from `scripts/lib/two-cluster-env.sh` as-is â€” it's already centralized and this task's Service creation makes it actually resolvable.
